Security Affairs newsletter Round 572 by Pierluigi Paganini – INTERNATIONAL EDITION
SECURITY AFFAIRS MALWARE NEWSLETTER ROUND 92

Critical Marimo pre-auth RCE flaw now under active exploitation

executive_brief: The threat landscape has entered a phase of accelerated collapse. In April 2026, the traditional window between vulnerability discovery and exploitation has effectively vanished. Anthropic's 'Claude Mythos' AI has autonomously identified thousands of zero-days across critical infrastructure, while adversaries like Storm-1175 are weaponizing flaws within 24 hours of disclosure. This AI-driven velocity is compounding a systemic supply chain crisis; major packages like Axios, CPUID utilities, and Smart Slider 3 Pro have been poisoned, eroding trust in the software ecosystem. Simultaneously, state-sponsored actors are pivoting from espionage to physical disruption, with Iran-linked groups targeting internet-exposed PLCs in US critical infrastructure and hacktivists compromising Venice's flood defense systems. The convergence of AI-accelerated attacks, supply chain fragility, and critical infrastructure targeting demands an immediate shift from reactive patching to resilience-based architecture.

threat_stories.headline: The Zero-Day Collapse: AI Accelerates Exploitation to Minutes
threat_stories.narrative:
The timeline for vulnerability remediation has shattered. Anthropic's Project Glasswing, powered by the Claude Mythos AI model, has autonomously discovered thousands of high-severity vulnerabilities across major operating systems and browsers, including a 27-year-old flaw in OpenBSD [125]. This defensive capability highlights the offensive potential available to adversaries. Attackers are already leveraging AI to compress the kill chain. The Storm-1175 group, linked to Medusa ransomware, exploits zero-days and N-day vulnerabilities within 24 hours of disclosure, transitioning from initial access to ransomware deployment at unprecedented speed [70]. 

This velocity is evident in the Marimo incident. A critical Remote Code Execution (RCE) vulnerability (CVE-2026-39987) in the Marimo Python notebook was exploited in the wild within 10 hours of public disclosure [271]. Similarly, the Flowise AI agent builder suffered active exploitation of a maximum-severity flaw (CVE-2025-59528) allowing arbitrary code execution [69]. The GrafanaGhost campaign demonstrated that AI-powered dashboards are vulnerable to zero-click data exfiltration via indirect prompt injection, tricking models into embedding sensitive data into outbound image requests [88]. The patch window is no longer days or weeks; it is hours.

timeline.event: Storm-1175 identified deploying Medusa ransomware within 24 hours of flaw disclosure
timeline.significance: Establishes new baseline for ransomware weaponization speed

timeline.event: Flowise CVE-2025-59528 confirmed under active exploitation
timeline.significance: AI agent platforms become high-value RCE targets

timeline.event: Marimo CVE-2026-39987 exploited within 10 hours of disclosure
timeline.significance: Demonstrates automated scanning and weaponization pipelines

threat_stories.impact_assessment: Critical. Organizations relying on open-source AI tools, data notebooks, and visualization platforms face immediate compromise risk. Patching is often impossible within the exploitation window.
threat_stories.action_required: Isolate Marimo and Flowise instances from public networks immediately. Disable CustomMCP nodes in Flowise. Implement strict egress controls for AI agent web browsing. Prioritize patching for CVE-2026-39987 and CVE-2025-59528 over standard patch cycles.

threat_stories.headline: Supply Chain Bloodbath: Axios, CPUID, and the Erosion of Trust
threat_stories.narrative:
The software supply chain is bleeding. On April 5, researchers confirmed a supply chain compromise of the Axios NPM package via a hijacked maintainer account, enabling dependency injection against downstream applications [1]. This was not an isolated incident. By April 10, the CPUID website was hijacked to distribute malware via trojanized downloads of popular utilities like CPU-Z and HWMonitor [295]. Simultaneously, the Smart Slider 3 Pro plugin for WordPress and Joomla was poisoned, with a backdoored update (version 3.5.1.35) distributed to over 800,000 active installations [250]. 

These attacks share a common vector: the compromise of trusted update mechanisms and developer accounts. The Axios attack specifically targeted the npm trust infrastructure, while the CPUID breach leveraged a compromised website to serve malicious executables [316]. The Smart Slider attack utilized compromised Nextend servers to push the malicious update [250]. This indicates a coordinated effort to poison the foundation of software development and maintenance, forcing organizations to verify package integrity manually rather than relying on registry trust.

timeline.event: Axios NPM package maintainer account compromised
timeline.significance: Direct injection into widely used JavaScript dependency

timeline.event: Smart Slider 3 Pro update hijacked via Nextend servers
CPUID website hijacked to distribute STX RAT

threat_stories.impact_assessment: High. Developers and IT administrators face compromised development environments and end-user systems. Trust in public repositories and vendor update channels is critically degraded.
threat_stories.action_required: Audit all npm dependencies and pin versions. Verify checksums for all software downloads from CPUID and Nextend. Revert Smart Slider 3 Pro to version 3.5.1.34 or earlier. Implement strict package signing verification.

threat_stories.headline: Critical Infrastructure Under Siege: Iran Targets PLCs and Venice Pumps
threat_stories.narrative:
State-sponsored actors are moving from data theft to physical disruption. Iran-linked APT groups, including CyberAv3ngers, are actively exploiting internet-exposed Programmable Logic Controllers (PLCs) in US critical infrastructure [135]. Censys identified 5,219 exposed Rockwell Automation PLCs, primarily in the US, responding to EtherNet/IP without authentication [318]. Attackers use these exposures to manipulate project files, alter SCADA data, and cause operational disruptions in energy and water sectors [147]. 

The threat extends beyond industrial control systems. On April 12, hacktivists claimed administrative access to Venice's San Marco flood risk reduction system, demonstrating the ability to view valve states and control panels [327]. This breach highlights the convergence of IT and OT security, where internet-exposed operational technology lacks adequate segmentation. The FBI and CISA issued joint advisories warning of these attacks, noting that attackers establish command and control via specific ports like 44818 and 502 [145].

timeline.event: FBI/CISA advisory issued on Iran-linked PLC targeting
timeline.significance: Official warning of active exploitation of OT devices
Quantifies the scale of internet-exposed OT infrastructure

timeline.event: Hackers claim control over Venice flood pumps
timeline.significance: Proof of concept for physical infrastructure disruption

threat_stories.impact_assessment: Critical. Direct risk to physical safety and operational continuity in energy, water, and municipal sectors. Potential for catastrophic physical damage.
threat_stories.action_required: Scan for internet-exposed Rockwell and Siemens PLCs. Block traffic on ports 44818, 502, and 2222 from public internet. Isolate OT networks from IT networks. Disable remote management on PLCs where possible.

threat_stories.headline: Ransomware Velocity: Storm-1175 and the 24-Hour Kill Chain
threat_stories.narrative:
The Medusa ransomware affiliate, Storm-1175, has redefined the speed of ransomware operations. Microsoft reports that this group exploits over 16 vulnerabilities across enterprise products, chaining zero-days and N-days to achieve full compromise within 24 hours [70]. Unlike traditional campaigns that dwell for weeks, Storm-1175 moves from initial access to encryption rapidly, leaving defenders with minimal detection time. 

This group targets healthcare and education sectors in the UK, US, and Australia [15]. They leverage fresh bugs and zero-days, sometimes weaponizing them before a patch is even available [50]. The speed is enabled by automated scanning and pre-packaged exploit kits. The financial impact is significant, with the group demanding high ransoms and exfiltrating data before encryption [72]. This velocity renders traditional patch management cycles ineffective, as the vulnerability is exploited before the patch can be deployed.

timeline.event: Storm-1175 identified deploying Medusa ransomware within 24 hours
timeline.significance: New benchmark for ransomware dwell time

timeline.event: Microsoft links Medusa affiliate to zero-day attacks
timeline.significance: Confirms state-level sophistication in ransomware operations

timeline.event: Medusa group reported exploiting zero-days for rapid deployment
timeline.significance: Confirms use of unpatched vulnerabilities in attacks

threat_stories.impact_assessment: Critical. Healthcare and education sectors face immediate risk of operational shutdown. Traditional incident response timelines are insufficient.
threat_stories.action_required: Implement network segmentation to slow lateral movement. Maintain offline backups. Monitor for Medusa ransomware indicators and Storm-1175 TTPs. Prioritize patching for internet-facing assets.

threat_stories.headline: The Zero-Day Firestorm: Adobe and Fortinet Under Active Attack
threat_stories.narrative:
Two critical zero-day vulnerabilities are driving a wave of active exploitation. Adobe Reader is under sustained attack via a zero-day (CVE-2026-34621) that has been exploited for months via malicious PDFs [195]. The vulnerability allows attackers to execute privileged APIs and potentially escape the sandbox [235]. Simultaneously, Fortinet's FortiClient Enterprise Management Server (EMS) is being targeted via CVE-2026-35616, an improper access control flaw allowing unauthenticated remote code execution [7]. 

CISA has added the Fortinet flaw to its Known Exploited Vulnerabilities catalog, ordering federal agencies to patch by Friday [41]. The Adobe vulnerability is particularly dangerous because it targets a ubiquitous application used by almost every employee. Attackers are using malicious PDFs to profile targets and exfiltrate data [251]. The Fortinet flaw allows attackers to bypass authentication via API, potentially leading to full control over managed endpoints [114]. Both vulnerabilities require immediate emergency patching.

timeline.event: Fortinet releases emergency patch for CVE-2026-35616
timeline.significance: Vendor confirms active exploitation in the wild

timeline.event: CISA orders federal agencies to patch Fortinet flaw
Adobe patches CVE-2026-34621 after months of exploitation

threat_stories.impact_assessment: Critical. Widespread risk across enterprise endpoints (Adobe) and security management infrastructure (Fortinet). Potential for complete network compromise.
threat_stories.action_required: Patch Adobe Reader and FortiClient EMS immediately. Disable JavaScript in Adobe Reader settings as a temporary mitigation. Restrict network access to FortiClient EMS management interfaces.

actor_profiles.name: Storm-1175
actor_profiles.recent_activity: Exploiting zero-days and N-day vulnerabilities to deploy Medusa ransomware within 24 hours of disclosure. Targeting healthcare and education sectors.

actor_profiles.name: DPRK-Nexus
actor_profiles.recent_activity: Executed a $285M theft from Drift Protocol via a six-month social engineering operation. Targeting Node.js maintainers and spreading malicious npm packages.

actor_profiles.name: Iran-Nexus
actor_profiles.recent_activity: Targeting internet-exposed PLCs (Rockwell, Siemens) in US critical infrastructure. Manipulating SCADA data and project files to disrupt operations.

actor_profiles.name: APT28
actor_profiles.recent_activity: Hijacking home and small-office routers (MikroTik, TP-Link) to conduct DNS hijacking and Adversary-in-the-Middle attacks. Targeting Microsoft 365 logins.

actor_profiles.name: UNC6783
actor_profiles.recent_activity: Stealing corporate Zendesk support tickets and using fake Okta pages in corporate breach campaigns. Targeting BPOs.

critical_vulnerabilities.description: Zero-day vulnerability allowing remote code execution via malicious PDFs. Exploited for months prior to patch.
critical_vulnerabilities.remediation: Update Adobe Reader to the latest patched version immediately.

critical_vulnerabilities.description: Improper access control allowing unauthenticated remote code execution via API.
critical_vulnerabilities.remediation: Apply emergency hotfix for EMS 7.4.5 and 7.4.6. Upgrade to 7.4.7.

critical_vulnerabilities.description: Pre-authenticated Remote Code Execution via WebSocket endpoint lacking authentication validation.
critical_vulnerabilities.remediation: Upgrade Marimo to version 0.23.0 or later.

critical_vulnerabilities.description: Code injection vulnerability in CustomMCP node allowing arbitrary code execution.
critical_vulnerabilities.remediation: Update Flowise to the latest patched version. Disable CustomMCP node.

critical_vulnerabilities.description: Critical code injection vulnerability allowing unauthenticated remote code execution.
critical_vulnerabilities.remediation: Apply the latest patch released by Ivanti immediately.

critical_vulnerabilities.description: Authorization bypass allowing attackers to gain root-level access to host systems via oversized HTTP requests.
critical_vulnerabilities.remediation: Update Docker Engine to version 29.3.1 or later.

critical_vulnerabilities.description: Remote Code Execution via Jolokia API management plane abuse.
critical_vulnerabilities.remediation: Upgrade to ActiveMQ Classic version 5.19.4 or 6.2.3.

critical_vulnerabilities.description: Memory overread vulnerability being actively probed by attackers.
critical_vulnerabilities.remediation: Apply available patches from Citrix immediately.

hunting_leads.title: Fortinet EMS API Abuse
hunting_leads.context: Attackers are exploiting CVE-2026-35616 via unauthenticated API requests to FortiClient EMS.
hunting_leads.query: index=fortinet_ems logs "API" AND ("unauthorized" OR "401" OR "403") AND src_ip NOT IN (trusted_admin_ips)

hunting_leads.title: Marimo WebSocket Exploitation
hunting_leads.context: CVE-2026-39987 allows unauthenticated access to the /terminal/ws endpoint.
hunting_leads.query: index=web_server logs "/terminal/ws" AND method="GET" AND status_code=200

hunting_leads.title: Malicious PDF Execution
hunting_leads.context: Adobe Reader zero-day (CVE-2026-34621) exploits JavaScript in PDFs for data exfiltration.
hunting_leads.query: process_name="AcroRd32.exe" OR process_name="Acrobat.exe" AND parent_process="Outlook.exe" OR parent_process="Explorer.exe"
indicators: Network connections from Acrobat to unknown IPs

hunting_leads.title: PLC Port Scanning
hunting_leads.context: Iran-linked actors scan for exposed PLCs on ports 44818 and 502.
hunting_leads.query: destination_port IN (44818, 502, 2222) AND protocol="TCP" AND src_ip NOT IN (internal_network)

hunting_leads.title: Supply Chain Poisoning
hunting_leads.context: Axios and Smart Slider updates were poisoned. Monitor for unexpected package versions.
indicators: npm install logs showing version changes

statistics.key_changes: Exploitation timelines have collapsed from days to hours. AI-driven vulnerability discovery is outpacing human remediation. Supply chain attacks are targeting update mechanisms directly.
emerging_trends: Ransomware deployment within 24 hours of disclosure
Supply chain poisoning of developer tools
